hero.title: H!NT Lab
hero.subtitle: Document Understanding · Human-Computer Interaction · Collaborative Cognition

labIntro.title: H!NT Lab
labIntro.p1: H!NT Lab (Human-Intelligence iNTegration Lab) focuses on exploring cutting-edge technologies for the deep integration of human intelligence and machine intelligence, dedicated to building more natural, efficient, and intelligent human-AI collaboration systems.
labIntro.researchIntro: Our research centers on three core areas:

areas.title: Document Understanding
areas.description: Deep research on multimodal document understanding, generation, and intelligent analysis

areas.title: Human-Computer Interaction
areas.description: Developing more natural and efficient interaction technologies and human-AI collaboration systems

areas.title: Collaborative Cognition
areas.description: Exploring synergistic mechanisms between human cognition and AI in complex tasks

labIntro.synergy: These three directions mutually support and synergistically develop: document understanding provides the core information processing capability, advanced interaction technologies facilitate smoother communication, and collaborative cognition research fundamentally optimizes the overall intelligence of human-AI symbiotic systems.

about.title: About
about.p1: Dr. Hao Wang is Lecturer (2019–2024) and, since 2024, Associate Professor in the Department of Intelligence Science and Technology, Shanghai University. He is currently seconded as Deputy Director of the Putuo District Data Bureau / Administrative Approval Service Center, Shanghai. He was supported by a China Scholarship Council (CSC) doctoral fellowship and received his M.Sc. (2014) and Ph.D. (2019) from Waseda University, Japan. He has worked at Microsoft Research Asia, Toshiba Global R&D, and NTT Communication Science Laboratories. He has published more than 40 papers in international conferences and journals such as ACL, EMNLP, COLING, and CogSci, of which more than 30 appear in SCI/SSCI/A&HCI-indexed venues or CCF-recommended conferences. He has led the Shanghai Municipal Science and Technology Commission “Sailing Plan” project (completed), an NSFC Young Scientists Fund project (at the AI interdisciplinarity interface), and five industry collaborative projects; as a key member he has contributed to multiple NSFC major and general projects, the National Key R&D Program, and social-governance special projects. He is a long-term PC member and reviewer for top AI/NLP venues including ICLR, ICML, NeurIPS, ACL, EMNLP, and AAAI, and for journals such as TKDD, TNNLS, and NLE. His main research areas are artificial intelligence, human–AI collaborative cognition, and natural language processing (including medical documents).
about.p2: We primarily welcome students with NLP backgrounds, and also those from linguistics, cognitive science, and psychology. International students are also welcome. Please note that Shanghai University currently offers Chinese-taught courses only, so international applicants are expected to hold an HSK Level 5 certificate.

projects.title: Research Projects
projects.subtitle: Major research projects ongoing in our lab

cards.name: Deepling.tech
cards.description: An AI-powered tech commentary self-media with a distinct personality, fully automated and known for its sharp, witty tech reviews.

cards.name: DeepTrans Studio
cards.description: Professional deep translation studio. An AI-powered high-quality translation platform supporting intelligent translation and post-editing of multilingual documents.

cards.name: DeepMed Search
cards.description: Intelligent medical literature retrieval system. Utilizing deep learning and knowledge graph technology to provide precise literature search and recommendation for medical researchers.

cards.name: ChineseLawTranslation
cards.description: Chinese law bilingual translation project. Building a high-quality Chinese-English legal terminology database to promote international dissemination of Chinese law.

Seek truth, stay grounded, and go far with steady steps.
